entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal memory layer that tracks user, session, and agent states across interactions. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval system and single-pass fact extraction to reduce latency while ensuring no critical data is lost during the memory lifecycle.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated memory system builds a self-wiring knowledge graph that automatically extracts entities and relationships from agent interactions. It features a unique hybrid search that boosts graph rankings with backlinks and exposes over 30 MCP tools for deep integration with Hermes and coding agents.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a 'retain, recall, and reflect' workflow that allows agents to learn and improve their long-term performance over time. It provides a simple two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory management and supports strict per-user isolation through custom metadata filtering.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a sophisticated library designed to maintain evolving state for users and groups, providing natural language querying to extract insights from deep interaction history. It serves as the foundational upstream library for several Hermes memory implementations and supports both Python and TypeScript SDKs.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness helps agents tackle complex tasks through an iterative self-improvement loop and automated strategy evaluation. It generates structured playbooks and traces for knowledge persistence, offering native integrations for Hermes Agent and MCP-compatible clients.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: This plugin provides lossless context management for Hermes using a hierarchical DAG-based engine that ensures no message is ever truly lost. It features three-level escalation for guaranteed convergence and utilizes immutable SQLite storage for profile-scoped, session-filtered conversation history.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project enables private, cross-session memory for Hermes Agent by providing a self-hosted backend for the Honcho library. It includes automated setup scripts for Docker and PostgreSQL, allowing users to maintain full control over their memory data using local or cloud LLM providers.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It employs a sophisticated hybrid retrieval method combining vector search, BM25, and causal graph traversal to share memory across different agent runtimes.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: Focused on efficiency, ContextPilot optimizes long-context inference by reordering and deduplicating context to maximize prefix reuse. It can reduce prompt tokens by up to 36% in agentic workloads across major engines like vLLM, SGLang, and llama.cpp.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format, facilitating memory exchange between different multi-agent systems. It uses hybrid BM25 and embedding search to automate memory injection for Hermes and other coding agents with zero API costs.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing codebases, documents, and media with automatic summarization and OCR capabilities. It uses edge tags to create bidirectional relationships between notes, making information easily navigable for agents via MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency, sub-millisecond memory system that uses a three-tier BEAM architecture to manage working, episodic, and scratchpad memory. Built on SQLite with native vector search, it offers high-performance local storage for Hermes agents without network overhead.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs to predict future information needs. Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, it uses local-first storage via SQLite-vec and provides an MCP-native server for seamless client configuration.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It is essential for Hermes agents performing security audits, cloud forensics, or malware analysis using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw enables collective skill evolution by automating deduplication and quality improvements through background loops. It serves as a unifying layer that integrates Hermes with other agents and devices to maintain a consistent, evolving library.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. It uses a multi-tier word replacement system and a two-pass audit to ensure Hermes produces human-like, undetectable prose.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that provides modular skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI. It allows Hermes to quickly adopt specialized professional frameworks compatible with the agentskills.io ecosystem.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills open format specifically for Pydantic AI. It optimizes Hermes' performance by using progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token consumption.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language, including ML models with tensor annotations. It features a self-correcting iterative loop that automatically fixes layout and style issues during the export process.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches the agent to write its own analytical prompts and identify structural conservation laws within complex codebases. It enhances Hermes' self-awareness by generating constraint reports that highlight analysis blind spots and limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink's oracle network, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It includes a local A/B testing framework to help developers evaluate and refine how Hermes handles blockchain data.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill allows Hermes to weave runnable code and prose together into synchronized markdown files. It enables the agent to extract source files from documentation and automatically sync edits back to the primary document.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides a shared memory system that allows Hermes instances to remember past work and extract training data for model fine-tuning. It is designed to help an agent document its own decision-making process to eventually train its own replacement.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with recursive dependency resolution. It uniquely includes commerce rails, allowing for the purchase and sale of skills using USD or the TON blockchain.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These are the official image generation skills for the FLUX model family, providing precise prompting guidelines and API integration patterns. They allow Hermes to handle complex visual tasks using JSON structured prompting and exact hex color specifications.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skill patterns. It streamlines development by generating both documentation and Python scaffolds directly from the agent's successful actions.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that manages structured memory and cross-feature learning through a plan-approve-execute cycle. It persists agent state locally, allowing Hermes to hand off complex missions to other models via markdown briefs.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill focuses on the autonomous execution of multi-step tasks, providing real-time progress tracking and checkpoints. It ensures reliability for Hermes by including built-in rollback procedures for safe recovery during long-running operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading architecture to minimize token usage during tool discovery. It maintains persistent server connections via a daemon, allowing Hermes to hot-reload configurations without service interruptions.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing code repositories and developer discourse into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant skills. It provides a verifiable audit trail with file-and-line provenance to ensure Hermes uses accurate, grounded technical knowledge.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub is a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills, featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes an agent-to-agent feedback system to establish trust and reliability scores for shared skills.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem, allowing C# developers to distribute skills as MCP resources. It supports progressive disclosure via YAML frontmatter to keep Hermes' context window efficient.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill connects Hermes to Nextcloud instances to manage files, calendars, and contacts via standard WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It provides a secure, guided setup for handling personal productivity data within the agent's workflow.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages for a variety of AI coding agents, including Hermes, ensuring developers always have access to the latest builds. It features a unified proxy API for cross-provider compatibility and supports multi-agent orchestrators to manage complex, parallel workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security environments by implementing hardware-enforced sandboxing through the NVIDIA OpenShell kernel. It offers multi-layer protection that blocks unauthorized network egress and filesystem writes using pre-configured security policy presets.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes deployments, covering everything from systemd services to secure secret handling via sops-nix. It is ideal for users seeking a reproducible setup that supports both Nix-managed skills and interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent comes pre-loaded with 100 tools and local model support via LM Studio. It eliminates the need for Docker or complex dependencies, offering a portable GUI-based environment with a dynamic tool maker for custom automation.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It is built for simplicity, supporting persistent state through mounted directories and allowing users to target specific Hermes tags during the build process.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a centralized Docker-first control plane for managing multiple agent instances and workspaces through a single web application. It features hierarchy-aware delegation between agents and automated task scheduling with integrated support for Telegram and WhatsApp.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production-ready headless environments, this repository configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is optimized for autonomous execution, enabling the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs without manual intervention.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal, allowing users to interact with the agent directly through a browser. It is designed to be deployed as a Portainer Git Repository stack, utilizing GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: A self-hosted orchestration platform that manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It features the Aegis review system to integrate human-in-the-loop sign-offs for dispatched tasks.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: This framework enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with native MCP support for extensible skills. It supports 23 model providers, including Hermes, and provides an interactive org chart to visualize team delegation.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A meta-optimization tool designed to refine harness code through an automated outer-loop search process. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations to track and improve performance frontiers for Hermes Agent.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: An agent-vs-agent arena where two autonomous AI companies compete for GitHub stars. It utilizes Paperclip and Hermes Agent to execute tasks, tracking progress via a real-time scoreboard and dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: A specialized pipeline coordinating 17 distinct agents focused on research, implementation, and infrastructure. The system ensures high-quality output by enforcing mandatory quality gates through dedicated reviewer and tester agents.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: An AI-native SDLC framework that guides Hermes Agent through an eight-phase autonomous development cycle. It leverages Supermodel MCP to provide structural code graphs and enforces architectural integrity via graph-diff phase gates.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: A delegation system that routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code using an ACP-style approach. It maintains clean context by isolating delegation workflows and implementing safety limits on timeouts and output length.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This high-performance Rust TUI provides real-time visualization of token consumption and costs across various AI coding agents. It is an essential monitoring utility for Hermes users who need to track cross-platform usage and manage expenses across multiple model providers.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This customization tool allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal interface using YAML-based themes and ASCII banners. By supporting partial skin definitions and inheritance, it enables the community to create diverse visual identities ranging from retro DOS to cyberpunk aesthetics.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 native skills directly into the Claude Code Channel, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth. It specifically caters to MLOps workflows by integrating tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control, this tool features persistent session management and MCP compatibility. It provides a critical supervisory layer for Hermes deployments, allowing humans to monitor agent actions with the ability to undo or rollback browser states.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework introduces per-folder scoping for Hermes, allowing developers to define project-local memories, tools, and instructions. It enables seamless switching between global settings and specialized agent configurations tailored to specific codebases.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A deterministic static linter that validates agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM costs. It helps Hermes developers maintain high-quality deployments by detecting structural failure modes and schema mismatches during CI/CD integration.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes by mapping personas, memories, and complex model configurations to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of workspace files and credentials, ensuring a smooth onboarding process for existing agent users.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This Docker-based deployment stack automates the setup of Hermes Agent alongside LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for a zero-cost local environment. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins, making it the fastest way to launch a fully-featured agent with local GPU inference.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This lightweight interface provides a three-panel layout that brings full functional parity with the Hermes Agent CLI to the web and mobile devices. Built with Python and vanilla JavaScript, it allows users to seamlessly browse workspace files and manage sessions without the overhead of heavy frameworks.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard designed for multi-platform management, offering unified configuration for eight messaging channels including Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. It features real-time chat with tool call expansion and detailed analytics for tracking token usage and costs across the ecosystem.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace acts as a multi-agent control plane, supporting role-based dispatch and swarm orchestration. It integrates a Monaco-based file manager and a dedicated memory browser, allowing users to search and edit agent context directly within a unified interface.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway for the Hermes stack that provides a browser-based terminal with full PTY support and a multi-agent status panel. It excels at administrative oversight, offering detailed token analytics and the ability to start, stop, and configure individual agent profiles.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: Tailored specifically for macOS, this native application enables remote management of Hermes Agents via secure SSH connections. It features a profile-aware terminal and allows for direct editing of remote files and skills with built-in conflict checks.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React dashboard operates as a Telegram Mini App, providing a terminal-style chat interface with slash command support. It includes system monitoring gauges for hardware usage and supports spawning up to five concurrent background agent instances.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop GUI that requires no admin rights or Docker to run local models via LM Studio. It comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools for TTS and ComfyUI, including a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers at runtime.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the onboarding process with guided installation and automated dependency resolution for Hermes Agent. It serves as a management hub for 16 different messaging gateways, persona editing, and scheduled task configuration.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: Focused on transparency and monitoring, this dashboard features a real-time activity feed and FTS5 search capabilities for inspecting full conversation histories. It provides a read-only view of configurations and a dedicated interface for managing scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that utilizes SSE streaming to display real-time tool execution timelines and agent responses. It includes an integrated marketplace for discovering MCP servers and skills, alongside profile-scoped management of global memory.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic web interface is built as a single-file React application, offering a sleek aesthetic for self-hosted AI assistance. It features workspace-aware file browsing and integrated terminal tabs for simultaneous shell access and log monitoring.
